content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 15, 1990


Gary M. Baum
Deputy City Attorney
City of Garden Grove
P.O. Box 3070
Garden Grove, CA  92642

                                    Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                         Our File No. I-90-468

Dear Mr. Baum:

     You have requested advice on behalf of Bart Blakesley, a
planning commissioner for the City of Garden Grove and candidate
for city council, regarding his possible obligations under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹  Because you have not asked for advice about a specific
pending decision, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance.²

                              QUESTION

     What conflict-of-interest standards of the Act apply to a
city council member who received a campaign contribution in excess
of $250 during his campaign for city council and while he was
serving as a planning commissioner?  Will the standards of Section
84308 apply?

                              CONCLUSION

     Generally, the provisions set forth at Sections 87100 et seq.
of the Act apply.


_________________________
¹  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

²  Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-90-468
Page 2

FACTS

Mr. Blakesley is currently a planning commissioner in the
City of Garden Grove.3 He has recently filed as a candidate for
city council. Mr. Blakesley may receive a campaign contribution
of more than $250 from a party three or more months after a final
decision is rendered by the Planning Commission in a proceeding
involving the party. If Mr. Blakesley is elected to the city
council, he will not serve as a planning commissioner.

ANALYSIS

City council members who are directly elected by the voters
are exempt from the provisions of Section 84308; Section
84308(a)(3).4 If Mr. Blakesley will be directly elected by the
voters, he will be exempt from Section 84308.

Mr. Blakesley will however remain subject to the conflict-of-
interest provisions that apply to public officials in general.
Those provisions are set forth at Section 87100 et seq. Section
87100 states:

No public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in any
way attempt to use his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to
know he has a financial interest.

Thus the Act examines the "financial interest" of the public
official in determining a conflict of interest. "Financial
interest" is defined by Section 87103. While "financial interest"
includes any source of income (Section 87103(b)), campaign
contributions are expressly excluded from the definition of
income. (See Section 82030(b)(1).) In the hypothetical posed by
your letter, the $250 campaign contribution would not cause a
conflict of interest for Mr. Blakesley in a later governmental
decision by the city council.

_________________________

3
This advice letter is based on the facts presented in your
letter of July 11, 1990, and our telephone conversation of July
25, 1990.

4
This exemption does not apply when a city councilmember is
sitting as a member of a nonexempt body such as a planning
commission or joint powers agency. Id. Thus if Mr. Blakesley is
elected to the city council and appointed to a joint powers
agency, the provisions of Section 84308 would apply to his
activities as a member of the joint powers agency.

Our File No. I-90-468
Page 3


I trust that this letter has provided you with the guidance
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Mark Morodomi
Counsel, Legal Division
